documents.title: University of Alabama at Birmingham public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: UAB's AI posture is health-research oriented, with graduate-research AI guidelines, biomedical informatics capacity, translational research infrastructure, and applied AI/ML in clinical domains.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: University of Alabama at Birmingham's public AI policy posture is cautious and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Center for Clinical and Translational Science
bodies.scope: UAB's AI posture is health-research oriented, with graduate-research AI guidelines, biomedical informatics capacity, translational research infrastructure, and applied AI/ML in clinical domains.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI guidance and governance
initiatives.description: University of Alabama at Birmingham has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires privacy, academic-integrity, and human-oversight controls as AI use scales.

initiatives.title: Cheaha GPU-enabled research computing
initiatives.description: UAB’s Cheaha documentation discloses GPU partitions for P100 and A100 workloads, including 72 P100 GPUs in public hardware tables.
initiatives.significance: This closes compute capacity with named cluster and public GPU evidence.

investment.description: No total acquisition or operating cost disclosed in the hardware documentation.

initiatives.title: Generative AI and UAB policy
initiatives.description: UAB’s AI policy page directs responsible use across instruction, research, and university activities and points to leadership principles plus practical recommendations.
initiatives.significance: This improves policy and assessment context for a previously thin AI guidance record.
initiatives.policy_implications: Applies responsible-use guidance across instruction, research, and university activities.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UAB Cheaha documentation lists GPU-enabled partitions including 72 NVIDIA Tesla P100 GPUs in the pascal generation and separate A100 GPU guidance for the amperenodes partition; the disclosed count is a public minimum for GPU capacity.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for AL. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: University of Alabama at Birmingham is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: UAB's AI posture is health-research oriented, with graduate-research AI guidelines, biomedical informatics capacity, translational research infrastructure, and applied AI/ML in clinical domains. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
UAB now has Cheaha GPU evidence and a $4.6M workforce grant, but Worker 3 did not find public enterprise AI platform seat counts or contract values.
UAB public sources did not disclose subgroup-specific AI student outcome measures.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
